title: 15-minute DNA extraction from beetle legs for PCR barcoding
author: Chase Mayers
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bmj4k4qw

steps.title: Prepare PrepMan Reagent
steps.instruction: Use a pipette to transfer 50 µL of PrepMan Ultra Sample Preparation Reagent into a small microcentrifuge tube. Label the tube appropriately. If possible, this should be done in a sterile environment and away from PCR equiptment to avoid contaminating the PrepMan reagent with unwanted DNA.

steps.title: Add Beetle Leg(s)
steps.instruction: Using sterile forceps, remove one leg (or multiple) from the beetle and transfer to the PrepMan reagant. Fresh, dried, and ethanol-pickled beetle specimens have all yielded usable results with varying success rates.

steps.title: Crush Beetle Leg(s)
steps.instruction: Using a sterile transfer needle (or other tool), thoroughly crush the leg(s) against the inner wall of the microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Incubate Sample
steps.instruction: Incubate the microcentrifuge tube at 100 °C for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Optional: Centrifuge and Transfer
steps.instruction: Optional: Centrifuge the microcentrifuge tube briefly and pipette the supernatant to a new microcentrifuge tube. Label appropriately.

steps.title: Store DNA Template
steps.instruction: The liquid in the tube is now ready to be used as template for PCR. It can be stored at -20 °C .
